mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where unemployment rises above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term returns are positive in >65% of declining industrial production months during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses the 200 threshold, inflation expectations force monetary authorities to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 200.0.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue streams, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows or payroll growth fails to predict positive returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and rising retail sales indicates unsustainable consumer leverage and inventory buildup, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive forward returns in >60% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in industrial production signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and impending earnings upgrades, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of periods where industrial production accelerates above the threshold.
